qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Recipe server accepts attacker-controlled local recipe paths and auto-imports template tools.py for code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a recipe-server code-execution bug caused by accepting an attacker-controlled local recipe directory and auto-importing template-local tools.py, while the advisory is a path traversal flaw in FileTools._validate_path() caused by checking '..' only after normpath() has collapsed it.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only qualifying finding is a recipe server local-template import RCE, not the FileTools._validate_path() path traversal bug described in GHSA-693f-pf34-72c5.
evidence: The advisory targets src/praisonai-agents/praisonaiagents/tools/file_tools.py, where _validate_path() normalizes the supplied path and only then checks for '..', enabling traversal to arbitrary files.

The only final qualifying finding targets POST /v1/recipes/run and code under src/praisonai/praisonai/recipe/* plus src/praisonai/praisonai/templates/*, where an attacker-supplied absolute recipe directory causes template-local tools.py to be imported and executed.
The finding report and PoC evidence do not identify FileTools, _validate_path(), path normalization order, '..' filtering, symlink handling, or arbitrary file access through file operations.
